data.title: Omeros: YARTEMLEA Cash Flows Funding High-Upside OncotoX-AML Pipeline, Supporting Buy Rating and $40 Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Brandon Folkes from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Omeros, with a price target of $40.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brandon Folkes has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to both Omeros’s commercial progress and its emerging pipeline. With YARTEMLEA already approved and on the market, he views the company increasingly through the lens of how effectively it can turn that product’s cash flows, and any potential collaboration proceeds, into value-creating R&D programs such as OncotoX-AML.

He highlights that, although investors currently attribute minimal value to OncotoX-AML, the new preclinical results in primate and xenograft models suggest a potentially transformative therapy in acute myeloid leukemia, with strong efficacy, mutation-agnostic activity, and a favorable safety profile. As Omeros advances toward IND-enabling work and a planned first-in-human study in 2027, he believes successful clinical translation of these data could unlock substantial upside that is not reflected in the present share price, supporting his Buy rating and $40 price target.

Folkes covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Cormedix, Omeros, and Enanta Pharmaceuticals. According to TipRanks, Folkes has an average return of 1.3% and a 40.17% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Entrada Therapeutics: Advancing ENTR-601-44 Positions Platform for Best-in-Class Dystrophin Restoration and Attractive Risk/Reward
data.description:
William Blair analyst Myles Minter has maintained their bullish stance on TRDA stock, giving a Buy rating on February 10.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Myles Minter has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the advancing clinical profile of ENTR-601-44 and the broader exon-skipping platform. The independent data monitoring committee’s decision to allow escalation from 6 mg/kg to 12 mg/kg in the ELEVATE-44-201 study signals a favorable safety and pharmacokinetic profile, which is particularly important given historical safety concerns seen with competing PMO approaches.

Minter also highlights that upcoming readouts from both the 6 mg/kg and 12 mg/kg cohorts represent major value inflection points, as they will test the potential for robust, possibly best-in-class dystrophin restoration. He believes Entrada’s EEV-based delivery and exon-skipping technology could generate stronger, dose-responsive dystrophin expression without being limited by receptor saturation, positioning ENTR-601-44 competitively versus Avidity’s del-zota and supporting a positive risk/reward skew for the stock.

Minter covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Dianthus Therapeutics, Neurocrine, and Moderna. According to TipRanks, Minter has an average return of 28.1% and a 60.65% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on February 10, Guggenheim also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $20.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
